case_title: SUNRUN INC. v. TRINITY SOLAR INC.

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
SUMMONS ISSUED TO PLAINTIFF SUNRUN INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE SEP-24-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT TRINITY SOLAR INC.'S APPLICATION TO ADMIT ALAN LEBENSFELD AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. APPLICATION IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TRINITY SOLAR INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR ALTERNATIVELY STAY PROCEEDINGS BASED ON AN EARLIER FILED LAWSUIT BETWEEN THE SAME PARTIES ON THE SAME CAUSES OF ACTION IS GRANTED. THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING THE RESOLUTION OF THE SUPERIOR COURT OF NEW JERSEY, CHANCERY DIVISION, MONMOUTH COUNTY CASE NO. MON-C-000074-21 (THE "NEW JERSEY LAWSUIT"). DEFENDANT'S UNOPPOSED REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.132. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-09-2021 Continued To Jun-29-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Alternatively Stay Proceedings Based On An Earlier Filed Lawsuit Between The Same Parties On The Same Causes Of Action
Off Calendar Nov-18-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Gil Walton, Esq., the Norton Law Firm, PC, 510-906-4900, gwalton@nortonlaw.com, for Plaintiffs, Sunrun Inc., et al,
Benjaimin A. Emmert, Esq., Littler Mendelson P.C., 408-998-4150, bemmert@littler.com, for Defendant Trinity Solar Inc. (ZOOM appearance)
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Court reporter,

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANT TRINITY SOLAR INC.'S Motion To Dismiss Or Alternatively Stay Proceedings Based On An Earlier Filed Lawsuit Between The Same Parties On The Same Causes Of Action is granted.
The action is stayed pending the resolution of the Superior Court of New Jersey, Chancery Division, Monmouth County Case No. Mon-C-000074-21 (the "New Jersey Lawsuit"). Defendant's unopposed Request for Judicial Notice is granted.

When an action in another state action has been filed covering the same subject matter as is involved in a California action, the California court has the discretion but not the obligation to stay the state court action. (Civ. Proc. Code sec. 410.30(a) [a court shall stay an action when it finds "that in the interest of substantial justice an action should be heard in a forum outside of this state."].) "In exercising its discretion the court should consider (1) the importance of discouraging multiple litigation designed solely to harass an adverse party, and (2) of avoiding unseemly conflicts with the courts of other jurisdictions. It should also consider (3) whether the rights of the parties can best be determined by the court of the other jurisdiction because of the nature of the subject matter, the availability of witnesses, or the stage to which the proceedings in the other court have already advanced." (Caiafa Prof Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal. App. 4th 800, 804; see Farmland Irrigation Co., Inc. v. Dopplmaier (1957) 48 Cal.2d 208; see also Gregg v. Superior Court (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 134.)

Defendants show that this action involves the resolution of issues that are substantially similar to those pending before the New Jersey court. It is undisputed Defendant filed the New Jersey Lawsuit before Plaintiffs filed this lawsuit and that action is still pending. (RJN, Exh. A, 28-35.) Moreover, Plaintiffs and Defendant are also parties to the New Jersey Lawsuit. (RJN, Exhs. A and B.) Lastly, both claims assert causes of action for declaratory relief requesting a judicial declaration that Plaintiffs' Confidentiality Agreements are unenforceable. (Compare RJN Exh. A, pp. 12-16 and RJN Exh. L, pp. 35-40.) Therefore, due to the substantial similarity of the theories and claims at issue in the two actions, there would be a real risk of unseemly conflict.

The Court rejects Plaintiff's argument that because the New Jersey Lawsuit does not assert the California Labor Code section 203 and the Section 17200 claim that Plaintiffs will be deprived of California law. Plaintiff's Section 17200 claim is derivative of other alleged claims rather than creating any independent claims. Regardless, Plaintiffs can elect to pursue all their claims in the New Jersey court rather than address some claims in New Jersey and some in California. Thus, to serve the interest of judicial efficiency, and to avoid the possibility of unseemly conflicts, the federal court should resolve the claims set forth in that action and then, if anything remains to be adjudicated in this case, the stay should be lifted so this Court can address the remaining claims. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.132.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)
